nodes.label: SupplyGuarantee dApp
nodes.rationale: Replaces paper-heavy, bank-intermediated trade workflows (letters of credit) with transparent programmable escrow: buyers fear paying upfront, sellers fear non-payment, banks charge high intermediary fees, and off-chain documents are unverifiable.

nodes.label: SupplyGuarantee.sol Escrow Contract
nodes.rationale: Entirely self-contained with no OpenZeppelin dependencies; implements its own SafeERC20Lite, OwnableLite, PausableLite, ReentrancyGuardLite to minimize attack surface and external dependencies.

nodes.label: Order Lifecycle Finite-State Machine
nodes.rationale: Strict FSM (Created -> Funded -> AdvanceRequested -> AdvanceApproved -> AdvancePaid -> InMilestones -> Finalized, with Disputed/Cancelled branches); each transition emits OrderStageChanged so the indexer can mirror state.

nodes.label: Milestone Lifecycle
nodes.rationale: Each milestone independently tracks NotStarted -> Planned -> PlanApproved -> Delivered -> InspectionApproved -> BuyerApproved -> Paid; when all reach Paid the order auto-finalizes, enabling milestone-by-milestone fund release.

nodes.label: Roles and Access Control
nodes.rationale: Six per-order roles (buyer, seller, carrier, inspector, bank, arbiter) plus owner; every function gated by a specific role modifier with no general-admin bypass; onlyConfigurator (owner OR buyer OR seller) governs order setup.

nodes.label: Document Anchoring (DocSlot)
nodes.rationale: Every trade document is anchored on-chain as a bytes32 SHA-256 hash in a DocSlot struct (hash32, timestamp, submitter), creating an immutable audit trail; each submission emits DocSubmitted.

nodes.label: Milestone BPS (Basis Points) System
nodes.rationale: Contract enforces advanceBps + sum(milestone.bps) == 10000 before lock, guaranteeing 100% of the purchase price is always accounted for; milestone amount = price * bps / 10000 at lock time.

nodes.label: Security Patterns (Lite Implementations)
nodes.rationale: Custom ReentrancyGuardLite on fund/bankPayAdvance/bankPayMilestone/_refund, SafeERC20Lite low-level call wrapper, PausableLite emergency halt, role modifiers, optional deadlines, SIWE nonce rotation for replay prevention.

nodes.label: SIWE Authentication Flow (EIP-4361)
nodes.rationale: Wallet-signature login instead of passwords: nonce expires in 5 minutes, nonce rotated immediately after verification to prevent replay, backend independently rebuilds the SIWE message rather than trusting frontend text, addresses stored checksummed.

links.note: subgraph.yaml targets contract 0xAA187d38D0226e1A4869ca596Fe43DcF3CaeE7D2 at startBlock 11038536, while README documents 0xfDCfA5454053db2Fc94D7568bE6e8854a3Cd8C28 and startBlock 10200000 - addresses disagree, possibly a redeployment not yet reflected in README
both use cryptographic verification to make off-chain artifacts trustworthy: document hashes anchored on-chain vs signed messages verified server-side
